How We Can Help

What Happened to You?

No two injuries are the same, and neither are the legal issues they raise. Whatever happened, we've handled it — and we know exactly how to build the case.

Car Accidents

Rear-end collisions, drunk drivers, hit-and-runs, and disputed-fault claims.

18-wheeler and commercial truck crashes involving trucking-company liability.

1

Free Consultation

Meet our team, ask questions, and learn how we can help — no cost, no obligation.

2

Investigation

We collect evidence, review documentation, and build the strongest version of your claim.

3

You Recover

4

Resolution

We negotiate hard for maximum value — and go to trial when it's the right call.

Why Choose Our Firm

The Difference Between Us and Everyone Else

Every injury firm in California claims to fight for you. Here's what that actually looks like once you're a client — at The Capital Law Firm versus the high-volume "settlement mill" firms you see on every billboard and bus bench.

What Matters
The Capital Law Firm
Typical Mega-Firm
Who handles your case
A real attorney, from day one
A rotating case manager — the attorney rarely calls
Insurance-defense insight
We used to defend insurers — now we use it against them

Trial readiness
Every case prepared to win at trial
Built to settle fast and move volume
Caseload per attorney
Kept deliberately low for real attention
High-volume caseloads, less time per client
Response time
Under 10 minutes, 24/7 — a person, not a queue

Fee structure
No win, no fee — always, no exceptions

Got Questions? We've Got Answers.

Frequently Asked Questions

As a California personal injury firm, we're here to help you understand the process and how our team can assist.

Our team has experience on both sides of the table — including defense work for insurance companies — which gives us insight into how carriers evaluate and try to minimize claims. We use that knowledge to fight for the maximum recovery on your behalf, and we never charge a fee unless we win.

A personal injury attorney investigates your accident, gathers evidence, communicates with insurers on your behalf, calculates the true value of your claim, and negotiates or litigates to recover compensation for your medical bills, lost wages, and pain and suffering.

If you were injured and someone else's negligence played a role — a careless driver, an unsafe property, a defective product — you likely have grounds for a claim. The best way to know for certain is a free, no-obligation consultation with our team.

Nothing upfront. We work on a contingency fee basis, meaning our fee is a percentage of what we recover for you. If we don't win your case, you owe us nothing — zero financial risk to finding out what your case may be worth.

We recommend against giving a recorded statement or accepting any settlement offer before speaking with an attorney. Insurance adjusters work for the other side, and anything you say can be used to reduce your claim. Let our team handle all communication on your behalf.

Seek medical attention first, then document the scene if you're able — photos, witness information, and a police or incident report. Avoid giving statements to adjusters, and contact an attorney as soon as possible. In California, most injury claims must be filed within two years, and claims against government entities can have deadlines as short as six months, so time matters.
